title: WKS Case Definition
description: A case type: the config-driven template for cases. Part of the WKS Case Configuration Standard. Self-contained (internal $defs only) so it validates identically across JSON Schema validators in any language.

id.description: Stable unique identifier for the case type (slug).

formKey.description: Key of the Form whose structure renders this case type's data.

stagesLifecycleProcessKey.description: Key of the BPMN process driving the stage lifecycle. Empty/absent when running engine-less (wks.bpm.engine=none).

deployed.description: Whether this case type is available to end users.

caseHooks.description: Event-driven automation: on a matching event, apply actions (stage/queue transitions).

bpmEngineId.description: Legacy field present in seed data. Not modeled on the engine's CaseDefinition and silently ignored on read. Documented here so the schema does not flag it; do not rely on it.

_id.description: Storage envelope field (e.g. MongoDB ObjectId wrapper). Not part of the logical config contract.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the WKS Case Configuration Standard this document conforms to. Absent/null is treated as the 1.0 pre-versioning baseline.

id.description: Stage identifier (string, typically the ordinal as text).

processesDefinitions.description: BPMN processes bound to this stage (manually or auto-started).

eventType.description: Event that triggers the hook. Vocabulary pinned to com.wks.caseengine.tasks.event.complete.CaseEventType.

processDefKey.description: BPMN process key the event must originate from.

taskDefKey.description: BPMN task key whose completion fires the hook.

newStage.description: Target stage. NOTE: holds the stage NAME, not its id (matches current engine + portal behavior).

queueId.description: Target queue. NOTE: holds the queue NAME despite the field name (matches current engine + portal behavior).

kanbanConfig.description: Free-form kanban board configuration. Known keys documented; extra keys permitted.

title.description: Comma-separated column titles (split on ',' by the portal).
